summary: In a smoker presenting with breathlessness, chronic hypoxemia secondary to COPD can trigger multifocal atrial tachycardia through heightened sympathetic activity and arrhythmogenic effects of poor oxygenation. Given its high association rate (∼60%) among patients with pulmonary diseases like COPD and typical presentation including dyspnea without cardiac symptoms, MAT is the most clinically supported diagnosis over myocardial infarction in this setting.
